danger_signals.visual_form: contrasting colored bullets/energy bolts against background
distinct enemy character designs separate from player ship
rapid background movement and spawn rates increase
danger_signals.meaning: approaching difficulty spike or boss encounter

danger_signals.visual_form: larger sprite scales, screen centering on entity, visual prominence
danger_signals.meaning: major threat ahead requiring sustained engagement

safety_signals.visual_form: absence of enemy sprites and projectiles in play area
distinct item sprites dropping from defeated enemies
safety_signals.meaning: resource gain and temporary safety window

safety_signals.visual_form: stage clear graphics or transition screen

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: distinct enemy sprite designs varying per stage
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: different enemy types with unique silhouettes per level

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: background tileset and color palette changes per level indicate environment shifts (sky, mountains, water, etc.)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: enemy spawn patterns and types escalate in complexity as stages advance

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: screen filled with moving sprites and projectiles creating dense visual field
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: rapid enemy fire, aggressive movement patterns, reduced safe space

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: unique large sprite design, centered screen focus, dramatic visual presentation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: boss executes complex attack patterns with multiple projectile types and movement states

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: multiple power-up items visible on screen simultaneously
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player temporarily gains enhanced weapon capabilities or defensive buffs

environmental_storytelling.example: each level features unique background artwork establishing distinct locations (sky domains, fortresses, elemental realms) through visual environment alone
enemy sprite designs match environmental theme of stage (sky enemies for air levels, mechanical enemies for fortress levels)

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: palette swaps between levels create visual progression through world geography without narrative exposition

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: smooth sprite movement following input, standard firing rate
enhanced weapon visuals (wider shots, faster fire rate), sprite may have visual effect overlay
predictable movement patterns, steady projectile fire in fixed directions
movement acceleration toward player, increased fire rate, spread shot patterns
established attack pattern, moderate projectile density, predictable positioning
attack pattern intensification, possible visual damage indicators (sprite flashing or darkening), increased projectile output

notes: Sky Blazer is a pure vertical shmup with minimal narrative elements. World state communication relies entirely on visual environmental design and enemy behavior patterns. No faction politics or political situations present in game design. Danger and safety signals are purely mechanical/visual rather than narrative.
